title: Podman container doesn't connect to multiple networks
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
Not sure if it's bug or additional feature though.
Docker supports connection to multiple network:
```
$ docker network create test1
bc1e8df612b28bd1986466b0d2fe97e09834bcc7c09c177d40962096d6afe257
$ docker network create test2
cc7f95d23fbddd854dfcc8d89cf6568519cef2fd2ff0ad39762cdfbd55183e6a
$ docker network ls
NETWORK ID          NAME                            DRIVER              SCOPE
18c8d06eedbc        bridge                          bridge              local
5a775f9a9c4f        host                            host                local
98a3288a99a8        none                            null                local
bc1e8df612b2        test1                           bridge              local
cc7f95d23fbd        test2                           bridge              local

$ docker create -it --name dockertest --network test1  alpine sh
56abc390eee0e322f5e89b831b249403863124f82ffdf438a22afbdd8eb48c4e

$ docker ps -a | grep dockertest
56abc390eee0        alpine                                   "sh"                       18 seconds ago      Created                                                                     dockertest

$ docker network connect test2 dockertest
$ docker start dockertest
$ docker exec -it dockertest sh
/ # ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
137: eth1@if138: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP
    link/ether 02:42:ac:17:00:02 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 172.23.0.2/16 scope global eth1
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::42:acff:fe17:2/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
139: eth0@if140: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP
    link/ether 02:42:ac:16:00:02 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 172.22.0.2/16 scope global eth0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::42:acff:fe16:2/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
/ #

```

Podman container connect to one only even if running with multiple `--network` options:

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo ./bin/podman run --rm --name test -it --network next --network custom alpine sh`

2.
```
/ # ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
3: eth0@if124: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP
    link/ether aa:30:1d:08:c0:81 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.89.0.10/24 brd 10.89.0.255 scope global eth0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::a830:1dff:fe08:c081/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

```

3.
```
114: cni-podman1: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether ca:d2:a3:49:82:7a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.89.0.1/24 brd 10.89.0.255 scope global cni-podman1
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::c8d2:a3ff:fe49:827a/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
116: cni-podman2: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state DOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether ca:34:40:70:1c:83 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.99.99.1/24 brd 10.99.99.255 scope global cni-podman2
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::c834:40ff:fe70:1c83/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
121: veth95c7f126@if3: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue master cni-podman1 state UP group default
    link/ether a2:56:71:4b:08:5a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff link-netns cni-dc9bde39-f51a-4a60-be43-1528768cd56a
    inet6 fe80::a056:71ff:fe4b:85a/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

```

**Describe the results you received:**
Podman connected to first created network only

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman connect to multiple networks, specified in CLI or with `network connect` as in `docker`.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.3-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.10
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.10
  podman version: 1.6.3-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.11.3
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.1-1.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.1, commit: 4346fbe0b2634b05857973bdf663598081240374'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 1914847232
  MemTotal: 25091493888
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-95.rc9.gitc1485a1.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc9+dev
      commit: 0840802d505e28d3adcbc7a275cbeadbdf64ddec
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 11718504448
  SwapTotal: 12645822464
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  eventlogger: file
  hostname: sshnaidm.remote
  kernel: 5.3.6-200.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    Executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    Package: slirp4netns-0.4.0-4.git19d199a.fc30.x86_64
    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.2
      commit: 19d199a6ca424fcf9516320a327cedad85cf4dfb
  uptime: 58h 43m 17.64s (Approximately 2.42 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/sshnaidm/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: fuse-overlayfs-0.6.4-2.fc30.x86_64
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.6.2
        fuse-overlayfs: version 0.6.4
        FUSE library version 3.6.2
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29
  GraphRoot: /home/sshnaidm/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 21
  RunRoot: /tmp/1000
  VolumePath: /home/sshnaidm/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

comments.body:
I think we expect `--network` to be passed only once, and network names to be provided comma-separated - might be a docs issue here.

`podman run --net net1,net2` for example

@mheon thanks! It worked:
```
$ sudo ./bin/podman run --rm --name tnet -it --network next,custom alpine ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
3: eth0@if143: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP 
    link/ether 52:7e:9b:b1:c3:97 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.99.99.5/24 brd 10.99.99.255 scope global eth0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::507e:9bff:feb1:c397/64 scope link tentative 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
5: eth1@if144: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP 
    link/ether 96:c6:5c:b7:84:65 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.89.0.12/24 brd 10.89.0.255 scope global eth1
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::94c6:5cff:feb7:8465/64 scope link tentative 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```
will try to submit a patch to docs.
